- Scale-DOWN still applies if tower overflows 92%

Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

Pivot edges now prefer host→host (not access→host) so BFS layer
assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

commit.message:
Add via_access_id provenance tracking to kill-chain graph

Schema v3→v4 migration adds via_access_id FK on credentials and vulns
tables, linking discoveries to the access session that found them.
State-server add_credential/add_vuln accept the new parameter.
State-viewer graph uses via_access_id for explicit edge inference
(access→vuln, access→cred) with heuristic fallback for NULL values.
Access nodes are pre-created before vuln/cred processing so references
resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix provided-cred chain ordering and reduce edge crossings

Provided-cred chain detection was fragile — parsed node labels to
find hosts. Now tracks hostsViaProvidedCred directly during access
node creation when matching against a provided credential. This
ensures attacker->cred->access->host->downstream ordering instead
of cred appearing as a sibling of the host.

Also broadened cred->access matching to check access.method for
the credential username (catches cases where username formats
differ between cred and access records).

Added barycenter heuristic for node ordering within layers (3
passes). Nodes in each layer are sorted by the average Y position
of their parent nodes, which reduces edge crossings — e.g., the
DC->gMSA_ADCS edge no longer has to route through credential boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix kill-chain ordering: provided creds flow attacker->cred->access->host

When a credential is provided (source matches "pentest", "provided",
etc.), the chain now flows: Attacker -> cred -> access -> host ->
downstream findings. Previously the host got a direct edge from
Attacker, making the cred appear as a sibling instead of an ancestor.

Changes:
- Skip attacker->host edge for hosts reachable via provided-cred chain
- Add access->host bridge edges so downstream nodes (vulns, pivots,
  blocked) that connect from the host node stay in the chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Prune dead-end vulns from graph, link provided creds from Attacker

Graph now only shows vulns that chain into something downstream
(credential, access). Dead-end findings that didn't lead anywhere
are pruned from the graph but remain in the Vulns table.

Credentials whose source matches "provided", "scope", "pentest",
"given", "initial", or "pre-engagement" now link from the Attacker
node instead of from a host — reflecting that they were supplied
as part of scope, not discovered through exploitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Render pivots as labeled edges instead of nodes

Pivots are relationships, not entities — they now render as styled
edges between source access/host and destination host. Edge label
shows method + status (e.g. "sshuttle", "chisel (identified)").
Exploited pivots are solid green, identified are dashed yellow,
blocked are dotted red. Removes the uninformative column of
identical "pivot" boxes.

Edge legend labels updated: Exploited/Identified/Blocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve kill-chain graph: subtitles, legend, manual refresh

Graph nodes now show two lines — label + contextual subtitle:
- Hosts: IP + role/OS
- Vulns: title + severity (color-coded by severity level)
- Credentials: user + secret_type (cracked)
- Access: user@host + type|privilege
- Pivots: method + source->destination (instead of generic "pivot")
- Blocked: technique + truncated reason

Adds a legend bar below the graph showing node shapes/colors and
edge styles (confirmed/pending/blocked).

SSE auto-refresh now only updates cards + tables. Graph only redraws
on explicit Refresh button click to prevent disruptive reloads.

commit.message:
Add state-viewer web dashboard with kill-chain graph

Read-only browser dashboard for engagement/state.db served at localhost:8099.
Stdlib-only Python HTTP server with inline HTML/CSS/JS — no dependencies.

Features:
- Kill-chain SVG attack graph (hosts, vulns, creds, access, pivots, blocked)
- State tables with sorting, filtering, collapsible sections
- SSE live updates (events every 2s, full refresh every 10s)
- Token-based auth for remote access (generate-token.sh binds 0.0.0.0)
- HMAC-signed session cookies, Bearer header support for API clients
- Graceful degradation when DB doesn't exist yet

Closes #64

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dashboard reliability fixes, JSONL agent discovery, event watcher timeout

Dashboard:
- Fix crash on embedded null characters in agent output
- Fix stale agents reappearing on dashboard restart (5-min mtime filter)
- Fix agent browser discovery when task symlinks are not created
- Discover agents from ~/.claude/projects/*/subagents/ JSONL dirs (reliable
  source, always created by Claude Code) in addition to task symlinks

  chains can take 15-25 min)
- Add credential-cracking-agent and pivoting-agent to SubagentStop hook

Orchestrator:
- Update dashboard path documentation to prefer JSONL paths from agentId

Tests:

delegate to onItemInsert when provided. Add deselect() to EditorControls

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add ClientPrivateGroup for JWT auth, HttpOnly cookies, and route security

Major security architecture improvements:

1. Router authentication restructure:
   - Create ClientPrivateGroup (JWT auth, no Casbin) for client-facing routes
   - Move all client shop routes (cart, address, order, payment, coupon,
     favorite, refund, review, notification) to ClientPrivateGroup
   - Move getUserInfo and setClientUser to ClientPrivateGroup
   - Only register/login remain on PublicGroup

2. HttpOnly cookie security:
   - Set HttpOnly=true on x-token cookie (prevents JS access)
   - Set Secure=true and SameSite=Lax
   - Remove js-cookie dependency from frontend source
   - Frontend now uses credentials:'include' for automatic cookie sending
   - SSR forwards cookies via request headers
   - Add checkAuth() to verify login state via API call

3. Frontend auth flow refactor:
   - app.vue: Use checkAuth() instead of reading cookie via JS
   - request.js: Remove manual token header, use credentials:'include'
   - auth middleware: SSR uses useCookie, client uses store state
   - userStore: Add checkAuth() and clearLocal() methods

4. Review/Coupon routes split:
   - Public read endpoints (getProductReviews, getReviewStat, getCouponList)
     remain on PublicGroup
   - Write endpoints (createReview, claimCoupon) moved to ClientPrivateGroup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
NAV-based stops + Kelly enforcement + directional tilt

Replace regime grid stopMultiplier with NAV-based per-leg stop system:
- Contract sizing: floor(NAV_HKD / 50K) × kellyFraction
- Per-leg stops: 1% NAV per side with directional tilt (2% total)
- Tilt shifts budget toward threatened side (e.g. MOD_DOWN → PUT 1.2%, CALL 0.8%)
- Removes hardcoded STOP_MULTIPLIER = 6 from agent routes
- Updates all callers: engineRoutes, agentRoutes, railsService, adapter

Trade #17 backtest: VIX 23 HIGH/MOD_DOWN → 4 contracts (Kelly 0.6),
PUT stop $1.70 (5.2x) survives $676 dip → +HK$2,714 instead of -HK$2,194.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: cleanup — remove old files, add dev proxy

- Delete old component files (Widgets, WidgetSettings, SkillsRunner, MCPTools, CommandPalette)
- Delete monolithic api-server.ts (replaced by api/server.ts)

commit.message:
Fix word-to-image alignment with proportional division

Replace fragile column-projection segmentation with proportional
ink-span division for word coordinates. The old approach found
noise regions at margins that shifted all words by one position.
New approach: find ink bounds per line, divide proportionally by
character count (RTL). Every word now gets a correct crop area.

commit.message:
fix: make home button work after canvas view swap refactor (#70)

* refactor: replace fullScreenCover with direct view swap for canvas

Canvas is now a peer-level view in ContentView instead of a modal
presented via fullScreenCover from DashboardView. This removes the
ContainerBackgroundSetter UIKit hack that was needed to fix the
black safe area behind the modal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make home button work after canvas view swap refactor

Move dev mode canvas auto-open from DashboardView.onAppear to
ContentView.onAppear. With the view swap (instead of fullScreenCover),
DashboardView.onAppear now fires every time the dashboard reappears,
causing an instant re-open loop when home button is tapped. ContentView
only appears once at launch, so the guard condition prevents re-opening.

Also add .contentShape(Rectangle()) to the home button for better tap
hit detection and debug print to verify taps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(clavain): include phase in SprintAdvanceIntent idempotency key

Key format changes from "session-sprint.advance-bead" to
"session-sprint.advance-phase-bead" to prevent collisions when the same
session advances multiple phases on the same bead.

Fixes: iv-zw1e6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add detailed [BAREMES] deferred block debug logs for dim change diagnosis

Logs now show: whether _baremeItem is set on each updateRow call,
the labor_modifiers JSON of the item, and the full override hierarchy
evaluation. This will reveal if the deferred block runs on dim changes
and why the price might not update.

commit.message:
docs: split into focused pages and update content (#291)

* Split docs into focused pages and update content

- Break docs/index.html (5600+ lines) into 7 focused pages:
  - index.html: Overview, How it works, Installation, Quick start
  - workflow.html: Workflow configuration, state types, error handling, hooks
  - cli.html: CLI command reference
  - multi-repo.html: Multi-repo manifest and Homebrew services
  - actions.html: Action reference (AI, GitHub, Asana, Linear, Git, Slack, Webhook, Workflow)
  - events.html: Wait event reference
  - examples.html: Example workflows

- Update all cross-page anchor links (href="#anchor" → href="page.html#anchor")
- Add per-page sidebars with correct local vs. cross-page links
- Preserve scroll spy behavior (local #anchor links tracked per page)

Content updates:
- Add settings block reference table documenting all fields:
  max_concurrent, branch_prefix, cleanup_merged, max_turns, max_duration,
  auto_merge, merge_method, container_image
- Add File layout section documenting XDG Base Directory Specification support
- Update erg clean description to list all cleaned resources
- Add erg start --once flag to CLI reference table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review feedback

commit.message:
security: add SQL identifier validation to retention policy pruning (#580)

Prevents SQL injection via table/column name interpolation in pruneTable().
While current retention policies use hardcoded values, this hardens against
future misconfiguration if policies become env-configurable.

Uses the same SAFE_SQL_IDENTIFIER allowlist pattern used throughout the
codebase (schema.ts, migrations) for consistency.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial implementation of pontoneer library

Provides mapping protocol (mp_length, mp_subscript, mp_ass_subscript) and
rich comparison protocol (tp_richcompare) support for Mojo Python extension
modules, backporting https://github.com/modular/modular/pull/5562.

Public API:
- PyTypeObjectSlot — slot tag constants
- NotImplementedError — signals Py_NotImplemented from rich compare handlers
- RichCompareOps — Py_LT … Py_GE constants
- PontoneerTypeBuilder — wraps PythonTypeBuilder with new def_method overloads

Includes a columnar DataFrame example demonstrating all four protocol slots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wraps inline cart drawer in <div id="shopify-section-flt_cart_1"> and

qty +/-, qty -, and trash-can remove actions.

commit.message:
Fix university tier placements to reflect actual CSAT cutoffs

실제 정시 입결 기준으로 전면 재정비:
- 중앙대 경영 → tier 2 (2등급 수준)
- tier 3: 서강대, 경희대 한의대/치대, 서울시립대 등
- tier 4: 건국대·홍익대·숙명·숭실·인하·아주 일반학과
- tier 5: 세종·단국·국민·지방거점국립대 일반학과

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bust Docker layer cache to ensure source files are always fresh

The GHCR-built images were serving stale COPY layers from GHA cache,
causing email.py and config changes to be missing from deployed containers.
Add CACHE_BUST build arg (set to git SHA) to force layer invalidation.
